info.name: Tourism Booking System API - Direct URLs
info.description:
Postman collection for the Laravel Tourism Booking System API.

How to use:
1. Auth requests use the full Railway production URL and direct sample credentials, so they work even without selecting an environment.
2. Run "Admin Login - Create Token" for admin-only endpoints such as package, promo package, tourist spot, booking approval, and payment management.
3. Run "Tourist Login - Create Token" for tourist booking/payment flows.
4. Login requests automatically save Bearer `token`, `admin_access_token`, `tourist_access_token`, and current IDs.
5. For file upload requests, choose a local file in the `image_file` or `proof_file` form-data row before sending.

Note: Requests use the full Railway production URL directly, so they work even if no Postman environment is selected.

Import this as a new collection and run this "Direct URLs" copy. Old imported Postman tabs/runs may still show `{{base_url}}`; close or delete the old collection to avoid using cached requests.

request.description: Creates a tourist personal access token using the sample tourist account. The token is automatically saved as `token` and `tourist_access_token` for protected tourist requests.
Creates an admin personal access token using the sample admin account. The token is automatically saved as `token` and `admin_access_token` for admin-only requests.

item.name: Create Extra Token

exec: pm.test('Extra token is created and saved', function () {

request.description: Requires an existing saved Bearer token. Creates one extra token for the currently logged-in account and saves its id as `token_id` for Revoke Token by ID.

item.description: Creates a second token using the currently saved Bearer token. This saves the new token id as `token_id`, which is the safe target for Revoke Token by ID.

item.name: List All Tokens

exec: const extra = body.tokens.find(token => token.name === 'Extra Postman Token');

request.description: Requires a saved Bearer token. Lists tokens for the currently logged-in account.

item.description: Lists personal access tokens for the current account. If an `Extra Postman Token` exists, its id is saved as `token_id` for revoke testing.

item.name: Delete Token by ID

exec: pm.test('Token is revoked by id', function () {

request.description: Deletes/revokes one personal access token by token ID.

item.description: Revokes the token id stored in `token_id`. Recommended flow: Login -> Create Extra Token -> Revoke Token by ID. This avoids revoking your current login token by mistake.

item.name: Delete All Tokens

request.description: Deletes all personal access tokens for the current authenticated account. Uses DELETE /api/tokens/delete-all. The old POST /api/tokens/revoke-all route still works as a compatibility alias.

item.description: Revokes every token for the logged-in account. After this, run Login again before calling protected endpoints.

item.name: Logout - Delete Current Token

request.description: Logs out by deleting/revoking only the current Bearer token.

item.description: Revokes only the current Bearer token. After this, run Login again before protected requests.

request.description: Public promo package list. Each record includes `image`, `image_url`, and `has_image`.

exec: pm.test('Promo package is returned', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To include an image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only create. Do not manually set Content-Type for file uploads.
Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To replace the image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only update. Uses POST with `_method=PUT` for multipart uploads.

exec: pm.test('Promo package is updated', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Promo package is deleted', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Admin token required. Deletes all promo package records. Keep `confirm` exactly as `DELETE ALL PROMO PACKAGES` to avoid accidental deletion.

exec: pm.test('All promo packages are deleted', function () {

request.description: Public tourist spot list. Each record includes `image`, `image_url`, and `has_image`.

exec: pm.test('Tourist spot is returned', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To include an image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only create. Do not manually set Content-Type for file uploads.
Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To replace the image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only update. Uses POST with `_method=PUT` for multipart uploads.

exec: pm.test('Tourist spot is updated', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('Tourist spot is deleted', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Admin token required. Deletes all tourist spot records. Keep `confirm` exactly as `DELETE ALL TOURIST SPOTS` to avoid accidental deletion.

exec: pm.test('All tourist spots are deleted', function () {

request.description: No token required. Public tour package request included here for a complete package workflow.
No token required. Public tour package request included here for a complete package workflow.

exec: pm.test('Tour package is returned', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

formdata.value: Sample package with uploaded image created from Postman.

request.description:
Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To include an image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only create. Do not manually set Content-Type for file uploads.

If this returns 401, send `Auth and Tokens / Admin Login - Create Token` first. This request uses `admin_access_token`.

formdata.value: Updated package details and image from Postman.

request.description:
Admin token required. Use Body > form-data. To replace the image, choose a local file for `image_file`; leave it empty for text-only update.

This request uses POST with `_method=PUT` because multipart form-data works reliably for optional image uploads. Do not manually add `Content-Type: multipart/form-data`; Postman sets the boundary automatically.

If this returns 401, send `Auth and Tokens / Admin Login - Create Token` first.
Admin token required. Deletes all tour package records. Keep `confirm` exactly as `DELETE ALL PACKAGES` to avoid accidental deletion.

Authenticated user required. If using an admin token, `user_id` is required because the admin must choose which tourist owns the booking. If using a tourist token, the API ignores `user_id` and uses the logged-in tourist automatically.
Admin token required. Deletes all booking records and their related payments. Keep `confirm` exactly as `DELETE ALL BOOKINGS` to avoid accidental deletion.

exec: pm.test('Payment is created or updated', function () {

request.description: Authenticated user required. Use Body > form-data. To include proof of payment, choose a jpg/png/webp/gif/pdf file for `proof_file`; leave it empty for text-only payment create/update.

exec: pm.test('Payment is marked paid', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Admin token required. Deletes all payment records. Keep `confirm` exactly as `DELETE ALL PAYMENTS` to avoid accidental deletion.
